# clang trunk: assertion "Must have at least one constraint" in TargetLowering::ComputeConstraintToUse during backend DAG for inline asm with malformed leading comma constraint

**Author:** Theaamadu
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/173675
**Status:** Open
**Labels:** backend:X86, crash, llvm:SelectionDAG, inline-asm, generated by fuzzer

## Body

## Compiler Explorer: https://godbolt.org/z/r7E47n1xr

- clang start crashing on `Trunk`
- generated by fuzzer


## Code
```c
int a[4];

int f1(int x, int y, int z) { return a[x] + (1 << y) + (100 / z); }

char *f2(int x) {
  char *p = (char *)__builtin_calloc(64, 1);
  p[x] = 3;
  return p;
}

int f3(int x, int *y, double z, double w) {
  int a[*y];
  if (x)
    __builtin_unreachable();
  asm volatile("" : : ",r"(&a[0]));
  return z / w;
}

int main() { return 0; }
```

## Code
```c
int a[4];

int f1(int x, int y, int z) { return a[x] + (1 &lt;&lt; y) + (100 / z); }

char *f2(int x) {
  char *p = (char *)__builtin_calloc(64, 1);
  p[x] = 3;
  return p;
}

int f3(int x, int *y, double z, double w) {
  int a[*y];
  if (x)
    __builtin_unreachable();
  asm volatile("" : : ",r"(&amp;a[0]));
  return z / w;
}

int main() { return 0; }
```
</details>


---

### Comment 2 - keinflue

Reduces further to
```cpp
void f(void* a) {
  asm volatile("" : : ",r"(a));
}
```

Crash since Clang 2.8, possibly unimplemented beforehand: https://godbolt.org/z/6s1cE3hfK

Does not occur with all types for `a`. E.g. `int a` and `long a` is ok and accepted by Clang.

Also, the leading comma is accepted by GCC, so probably not intended to be considered malformed: https://godbolt.org/z/74Pjf751d

---

### Comment 3 - keinflue

GCC seems to support empty alternatives in the constraints string. So I tried to implement this for LLVM and Clang as well.

LLVM parses the string and naively counts the alternatives at https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/1e985b6ddf023af5782d48c1cce881668fdf6ceb/llvm/lib/IR/InlineAsm.cpp#L82. Adjusting this parser is simple enough. However, there is validation code in Clang at https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/1e985b6ddf023af5782d48c1cce881668fdf6ceb/clang/lib/Sema/SemaStmtAsm.cpp#L603 which currently does the same and seems to be a bit more work, because the modifiers still need to be separated to detect whether the first `,` is preceded by an empty alternative.

So I'd like feedback on the direction before I invest some more time.

---
